key_insight: The paper reframes structured preconditioner design as a geometric distance-and-reachability problem: how far a restricted metric family is from achieving a target condition-number class.
engineering_relevance: This gives practitioners a certificate-based way to judge whether diagonal, block, or Kronecker preconditioners can meet conditioning goals before investing in expensive solver tuning.
method: Defines restricted dynamic geometric complexity as an intrinsic certificate distance under metric-family constraints.
Proves monotonicity, submanifold-distance principles, and exact/feasibility characterizations for diagonal and block cases.
Derives Kronecker and Hessian-relative certificate tests with normal equations, mismatch bounds, and solver convergence guarantees.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports deterministic toy and synthetic workflows that verify diagonal gaps, block primal/dual certificates, Kronecker spectral width, and Hessian-relative candidate certificates on small quadratic instances.
watch_item: Several results are diagnostic or certificate-oriented rather than full optimizer characterizations, so practical impact depends on how well the abstract geometric tests scale to large, noisy systems.
paper_title: Restricted Dynamic Geometric Complexity: Certificates for Structured Preconditioning
